document.documentType: Wireless module hardware and regulatory specification
standards: FCC Part 15 Subpart B Class B

sourceSummary.oneLine: Laird Connectivity specification for ST60-2230C-P/ST60-2230C-PU Wi-Fi and Bluetooth M.2 modules, including hardware interfaces, radio specifications, certified antennas, and regulatory information.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports OEM integration and regulatory documentation for host devices using the ST60-2230C module family.
keyPoints: The ST60-2230C-P/ST60-2230C-PU module is a dual band 2x2 802.11ac WLAN plus Bluetooth 5.1 dual mode adapter.
The module uses PCIe for WLAN and either UART or USB for Bluetooth depending on model.
The ST60-2230C holds listed certifications for FCC, ISED, MIC, KC, and SRRC jurisdictions.
OEM integrators must meet applicable user guide, labeling, antenna, and end-product compliance requirements.
The document lists EU RED reference standards and a Declaration of Conformity date of August 2020.

entities.evidence: This radio transmitter (IC: 3147A-602230C) was approved by Innovation, Science and Economic Development (ISED) Canada

entities.evidence: The 60-SOM is approved for use in the China market.

evidence.claim: The document is Version 1.26.
evidence.evidenceText: Version 1.26

evidence.claim: Version 1.26 is dated 3 Aug 2022.
evidence.evidenceText: 1.26 3 Aug 2022 Updated dimensions of module in Mechanical Specifications Added recommendation for Mounting screw

evidence.claim: The document describes key hardware aspects of the ST60-2230C-P/ST60-2230C-PU M.2 module.
evidence.evidenceText: This document describes key hardware aspects of the Laird Connectivity ST60-2230C-P/ ST60-2230C-PU M.2 module

evidence.claim: The document is intended to assist device manufacturers and related parties with module integration into host devices.
evidence.evidenceText: This document is intended to assist device manufacSQGturers and related parties with the integration of this module into their host devices.

evidence.claim: The ST60-2230C-P/ST60-2230C-PU module is a dual band 2x2 802.11ac WLAN plus Bluetooth 5.1 dual mode adapter.
evidence.evidenceText: The ST60-2230C-P/ST60-2230C-PU module is a dual band 2x2 802.11ac WLAN plus Bluetooth 5.1 dual mode adapter

evidence.claim: The module complies with the M.2 2230 E-Key standard.
evidence.evidenceText: it complies with M.2 2230 E-Key standard

evidence.claim: ST60-2230C-P uses PCIe for WLAN and UART for Bluetooth.
evidence.evidenceText: ST60-2230C-P – The interfaces are PCIe/UART (WLAN/Bluetooth) with configuration setting to 011.

evidence.claim: ST60-2230C-PU uses PCIe for WLAN and USB for Bluetooth.
evidence.evidenceText: ST60-2230C-PU – The interfaces are PCIe/USB (WLAN/Bluetooth) with configuration setting to 010.

evidence.claim: The module supports IEEE 802.11ac 2x2 MIMO with data rates up to 866.7 Mbps.
evidence.evidenceText: IEEE 802.11 ac, 2X2 MIMO spatial stream multiplexing with data rate up to MCS9 (866.7 Mbps).

evidence.claim: The module supports Bluetooth Class 1 and Bluetooth 5.1 Low Energy.
evidence.evidenceText: Bluetooth 5.1 (BDR/EDR/LE), Bluetooth Class 1

evidence.claim: The module regulatory domain support includes FCC, EU, ISED Canada, MIC Japan, and KC Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Regulatory Domain Support FCC EU ISED (Canada) MIC (Japan) KC (Korea)

evidence.claim: The ST60-2230C holds FCC ID SQG-60SIPT.
evidence.evidenceText: USA (FCC) SQG-60SIPT

evidence.claim: The ST60-2230C holds ISED ID 3147A-602230C.
evidence.evidenceText: Canada (ISED) 3147A-602230C

evidence.claim: The ST60-2230C holds Japan MIC ID 003-170090.
evidence.evidenceText: Japan (MIC) 003-170090

evidence.claim: The 60-2230C is approved for use in the Korean market under certificate MSIP-CRM-LAI-60-2230C.
evidence.evidenceText: The 60-2230C is approved for use in the Korean market. Model Certificate Number 60-2230C MSIP-CRM-LAI-60-2230C

evidence.claim: The 60-2230C has China CMIIT ID 2017DJ2473.
evidence.evidenceText: Model CMIIT ID 60-2230C 2017DJ2473

evidence.claim: For FCC use, the end product must be labeled with Contains FCC ID: SQG-60SIPT.
evidence.evidenceText: The end product must be labeled in a visible area with the following: Contains FCC ID: SQG-60SIPT

evidence.claim: The FCC authorization is no longer valid if the co-location condition cannot be met.
evidence.evidenceText: If this condition cannot be met ... then the FCC authorization is no longer considered valid and the FCC ID cannot be used on the final product.

evidence.claim: For Canada, the module may not be co-located with any other transmitter or antenna.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is intended only for OEM integrators under the following conditions: 1. The transmitter module may not be co-located with any other transmitter or antenna.

evidence.claim: The ST60-2230C is approved for use in the Japanese market.
evidence.evidenceText: The ST60-2230C is approved for use in the Japanese market.

evidence.claim: Japan MIC requires antennas not on the approved list to be added to the certificate before use in Japan.
evidence.evidenceText: If your antenna is not on the approved list, regardless of whether it is comparative, it must be added to the certificate before it can be used in Japan.

evidence.claim: For Australia/New Zealand, OEMs must verify final end-product compliance and all end-products require their own certification.
evidence.evidenceText: If this device is used in a product, the OEM has responsibility to verify compliance of the final end product to the Australia/New Zealand (RCM) Standards. All end-products require their own certification (SDoc).
evidence.pageTarget: Australia New Zealand RCM wireless module compliance

evidence.claim: The EU RED statements must be added in their entirety and without modification into the host device user guide.
evidence.evidenceText: the following Radio Equipment Directive (RED) statements must be added in their entirety and without modification into a prominent place in the user’s guide

evidence.claim: The EU Declaration of Conformity identifies Laird Connectivity as manufacturer and ST60-2230C as product.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer Laird Connectivity Products ST60-2230C

evidence.claim: The EU Declaration of Conformity date of issue is August 2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Date of Issue: August 2020

reviewFlags.reason: No explicit document title is visible in the provided OCR text; title field is left empty.

Source metadata authoritySignal is MFDS/domestic, but the source text is a Laird Connectivity wireless module specification covering FCC, EU, ISED, MIC, KC, RCM, and SRRC regulatory topics rather than an MFDS-issued document.
OCR contains visible recognition errors such as 'manufacSQGturers', which may affect exact wording in some passages.
Australia/New Zealand status appears mixed: current certifications table lists Australia and New Zealand as N/A, while later text states RCM pending and describes OEM SDoc responsibility.
China section states 'The 60-SOM is approved for use in the China market' but then lists model 60-2230C and CMIIT ID 2017DJ2473, creating model-name ambiguity.
ISED labeling passage uses placeholder text 'Contains IC: xxx' rather than the specific IC ID.
